cloud-architecture/gcp/index.description: Playrunner is designed to be easily deployable to Google Cloud Platform, providing a massively scalable, auto-scaling execution environment for your visual Playwright workflows.

contributing.title: Contributing
contributing.description: Playrunner is an early developer tool project for Playwright orchestration. The

integration-packages/environment.title: Environment Integration
integration-packages/environment.description: Configure reusable environment variables and inject them into Playrunner workflow nodes.

integration-packages/gcp.title: GCP Integration
integration-packages/gcp.description: Connect Google Cloud credentials and register GCP runner infrastructure for Playrunner workflows.

integration-packages/github.title: GitHub Integration
integration-packages/github.description: Connect GitHub repositories and install app credentials for Playrunner workflows.

integration-packages/index.title: Overview
integration-packages/index.description: Installable Playrunner integrations, package structure, SDK usage, and persistence model.

integration-packages/javascript.title: JavaScript Integration
integration-packages/javascript.description: Run custom JavaScript inside Playrunner workflow nodes.

integration-packages/jira.title: Jira Integration
integration-packages/jira.description: Create and sync Jira tickets from Playrunner workflows.

integration-packages/playwright.description: Trigger Playwright test runs from Playrunner workflows.

integration-packages/schedule.title: Schedule Integration
integration-packages/schedule.description: Start Playrunner workflows on recurring schedules.

local-dev/docker-images.title: Docker Images
local-dev/docker-images.description: Local development only. Both the Orchestrator and Playwright runner run as Docker images that must be built on your local machine before use.

local-dev/environment-variables.title: Environment Variables
local-dev/environment-variables.description: Local development only. Each service reads its configuration from environment variables. Defaults are designed to work out-of-the-box for local dev.

local-dev/index.title: Overview
local-dev/index.description: This documentation covers local development only.

local-dev/postgres-prisma-and-local-auth.title: PostgreSQL, Prisma & Local Auth
local-dev/postgres-prisma-and-local-auth.description: Local development only. The default local stack now runs a Docker-backed PostgreSQL database and a setup-seeded username/password login.

local-dev/remote-debugging.title: Remote Runner Messaging
local-dev/remote-debugging.description: Local development only. This describes how workflow messages move when a cloud runner is used while the Playrunner API is running on your machine.

local-dev/services-and-ports.title: Services & Ports
local-dev/services-and-ports.description: Local development only. The standard local-flow ports below come from the repo-root .env.local.example file and package .env.example files. Override them in .env.local or the relevant service .env.

local-dev/troubleshooting.title: Troubleshooting
local-dev/troubleshooting.description: Local development only. Common issues and how to resolve them.

local-dev/workflow-execution.title: Workflow Execution Flow
local-dev/workflow-execution.description: Local development only. This describes the full journey of a workflow run from the moment you click "Run" in the editor.

overview.title: Overview
overview.description: Playrunner is a visual workflow orchestration platform for running automated Playwright test pipelines. Connect triggers, integrations, and test runners by dragging nodes on a canvas — no glue code required.

roadmap.title: Roadmap
roadmap.description: Playrunner is early. The current roadmap is focused on making Playwright test

tutorials/connect-github.title: Connect GitHub
tutorials/connect-github.description: Playrunner uses a GitHub App with OAuth user authorization to clone private test

tutorials/create-your-first-workflow.title: Create Your First Workflow
tutorials/create-your-first-workflow.description: In this tutorial you'll build a simple two-node workflow in the Playrunner visual editor: an Environment config node feeds variables into a Playwright test run.

tutorials/getting-started.title: Getting Started
tutorials/getting-started.description: This tutorial walks you through setting up the complete Playrunner local development environment from scratch. By the end you'll have the local stack running, the workspace configured through the setup UI, and the product app ready for login.

tutorials/index.title: Tutorials
tutorials/index.description: Welcome to the Playrunner tutorials. These step-by-step guides take you from zero to running your first automated Playwright workflow.

tutorials/run-your-first-test.title: Run Your First Test
tutorials/run-your-first-test.description: With a workflow saved and GitHub connected, you're ready to trigger a live test run and watch it execute in real time.

tutorials/understanding-reports.title: Understanding Test Reports
tutorials/understanding-reports.description: Every Playrunner test run generates a full Playwright HTML report stored with a unique run ID. This tutorial shows you how to navigate the report, find failing tests, and access screenshots and traces.
